title: Houstonia bouvardioides
taxonomy: Houstonia bouvardioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Houstonia bouvardioides is classified within the kingdom Plantae. The genus Houstonia is part of the tribe Rubieae, which is a group of flowering plants that include the coffee, gardenia, and madder families. Houstonia bouvardioides is also known as the desert bluecurl.

intro: Houstonia bouvardioides, commonly known as the red Houstonia or Mexican bluet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Houstonia bouvardioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
